title: Value-Based Health Care
gist: This is a BCG service/landing page for its value-based health care practice, not an analytical article; it is mostly capability and client-work marketing. The one genuine signal worth filing is the thesis that healthcare spend and outcomes are decoupled, with a substantial portion of spending wasted, plus a few client-case data points.

insights.claim: BCG argues health outcomes vary widely across hospitals, regions, and countries irrespective of spending—those that spend the most do not necessarily deliver the best care—and a substantial share of healthcare spending is wasted on avoidable complications or unnecessary treatments.
insights.speaker: BCG Henderson Institute
insights.so_what: Frames value-based care as a structural cost-reduction lever—relevant to payers, hospital operators, and health-system reform exposure.
insights.quote: There is growing evidence that a substantial portion of health care spending is, quite simply, wasted on avoidable medical complications or medically unnecessary treatments.

insights.claim: A Dutch network of seven teaching hospitals using a value-based approach cut unnecessary inpatient stays by nearly 30% and reoperation rates due to complications in breast cancer patients by up to 74% within about eighteen months.
insights.speaker: BCG Henderson Institute
insights.so_what: Concrete magnitude of waste reduction achievable, supporting the cost-savings case for value-based models.
insights.quote: reductions of nearly 30% in unnecessary inpatient stays and up to 74% in the rate of reoperation due to complications in breast cancer patients

insights.claim: A European payer using BCG's Patient Finder platform identified use cases worth €300 million in potential cost reductions by mapping patient journeys and building prediction models.
insights.speaker: BCG Henderson Institute
insights.so_what: Illustrates the analytics-driven savings pool payers can target, relevant to health-data and payer-efficiency theses.
insights.quote: identify use cases with a total value of €300 million in potential cost reductions.
